rows.title: SKILL Body vs Reference Boundary Re-audit
rows.summary: v2.26.0 SKILL slimming exposed that Blake skips Layer 2/completion report because those rules are in references/ not loaded. Audit 36 reference files, inline execution-discipline content back into body. Both platforms must be verified.
rows.cost_rationale: 36 reference files to audit + edits to multiple SKILL.md files + dual-platform verification = large scope
rows.value_rationale: P0 item; Blake actively skipping critical steps due to this gap means quality chain is broken right now
rows.deliverable: Updated alex/blake SKILL.md files with execution-discipline protocols (Gate 3 checklist, Layer 2 requirements, completion format) inlined back into body; passing Gate 3+4 on both Claude Code and Codex platforms; principle entry in principles.md confirmed

rows.title: Surplus Burn Mode Phase 2: Budget-Loop Auto-Execution + Safety Routing + Dogfood
rows.summary: Build the auto-execution half of *surplus: budget loop picks safe-to-YOLO candidates from the Phase 1 plan, runs each through yolo-epic, skips SAFETY items, and writes a unified acceptance digest. Requires a live dogfood run as final acceptance.
rows.cost_rationale: New workflow file, budget-loop orchestration via yolo-epic.workflow.js, circuit-breaker logic, unified acceptance digest, and a full dogfood run — L-tier effort.
rows.value_rationale: Turns idle Claude quota into shipped deliverables automatically; Phase 1 JSON sidecar is already the contract, so the execution engine has a clear, ready input.
rows.deliverable: surplus-execute.workflow.js with budget-guarded loop; SURPLUS-REPORT-{date}.md digest from a live dogfood run that completes >=1 real backlog task end-to-end and archives it; SAFETY-tagged candidates routed to needs-human list (never auto-executed)

rows.title: O1/KR2 Revised Differentiator Set — Document Evidence-Grounded Replacement Positioning
rows.summary: The 2026-06-09 repositioning research (3-walls stress test + Round 2 enrichment) falsified all three original TAD differentiators and produced a new evidence-backed positioning: 'TAD forces the friction that lets a person WITH baseline judgment safely operate across unfamiliar domains while accumulating cheaper-next-pass residue.' This revised set has never been written into OBJECTIVES.md or a differentiator doc. Item #9 on the backlog validates the OLD differentiators via head-to-head comparison — this is distinct: documenting the REPLACEMENT set derived from existing research.
rows.cost_rationale: Single synthesis document from already-complete research; reads two existing findings files and writes one output — no new research or code.
rows.value_rationale: O1/KR2: the 'identified and validated' differentiators are currently misrepresented in OBJECTIVES.md (pointing to 3 claims that research has since falsified); this write-up corrects the record with evidence-grounded replacements, which is a prerequisite before any README rewrite or Epic investment in differentiator-amplifying work.
rows.deliverable: .tad/evidence/research/o1-kr2-revised-differentiator-set.md containing: (1) the 3 falsified original differentiators with the specific evidence that falsified each, (2) the 2 surviving evidence-backed differentiators (beneficial friction gates + single-operator compounding residue) with source citations, (3) the honest defensible positioning statement verbatim from the findings file, (4) the 'decision-grade implication' re: mechanical enforcement gate decision. OBJECTIVES.md KR2 comment updated to reference this file.

rows.title: Close Edit-Time Parity Drift Window — Blake Completion Parity Check
rows.summary: Add a parity check to Blake's completion protocol: after any handoff touching .claude/skills/, run release-verify.sh parity; on drift auto-run parity --fix and include the sync in the completion commit. Closes the gap between edit-time and *publish-time that left Codex running stale protocols.
rows.cost_rationale: Wiring-only change to blake/SKILL.md + one script call — single-file protocol edit under 30k tokens, but touches SAFETY-adjacent SKILL.md.
rows.value_rationale: Directly closes a documented residual gap (Gate 4 of codex-parity-step3b), high confidence since the parity tool is already hardened.
rows.deliverable: blake/SKILL.md completion protocol updated with parity check step + release-verify.sh parity --fix wiring + verified run showing zero drift after a simulated skill edit

rows.title: O2/KR1 Capability Pack Behavioral Examples — Per-Pack examples/ Fixture Schema + 2 Pilot Packs
rows.summary: The YOLO audit (principles.md SAFETY entry) identified 'validation theater' as P0: structural checks prove files exist but do not prove pack improves agent behavior. The recommended fix (3-5 before/after task comparisons with fixed rubric) has never been implemented as a concrete per-pack artifact schema. IDEA-20260527-pack-behavioral-examples captures the idea but it is absent from the backlog as an execution item. This delivers the schema + two pilot packs with populated fixtures.
rows.cost_rationale: Three files touched: schema template, two pack examples/ directories populated, one SKILL.md line added — no new abstractions, but fixture content requires careful authoring.
rows.value_rationale: O2/KR1: directly surfaces an upgrade direction from YOLO cross-model audit (Codex 3/5, Gemini 2/5 on validation); closes the 'structural checks prove nothing behavioral' gap that both reviewers independently rated as the highest-severity finding, making capability pack evaluation genuinely discriminative.
rows.deliverable: .tad/templates/pack-behavioral-examples-schema.md defining the fixture format (scenario-input.md + scenario-expected-output.md pairs, ≥2 per pack, with 5-dimension rubric header); .claude/skills/code-security/examples/ and .claude/skills/web-frontend/examples/ each populated with 2 fixture pairs; capability-upgrade SKILL.md updated to require examples/ as mandatory Gate 2 deliverable for any new pack.

rows.title: IDEA: Structured Feedback Collector HTML Interface
rows.summary: IDEA-20260610: HTML-based human judgment interface for non-code artifacts (frontend pages, audio, video, brands). AI generates artifact + review interface; human gives structured feedback via HTML; JSON flows back to AI. Proven in Colin voice project.
rows.cost_rationale: New HTML component + JS + integration with existing schema + dogfood cycle; substantial frontend work
rows.value_rationale: High value for non-dev TAD usage; already proven in Colin project; complements existing feedback_collector_protocol in Blake SKILL
rows.deliverable: feedback-collector.html template + JavaScript JSON serializer; integration with existing feedback-json-schema.md v1.0; dogfood on ≥1 non-code artifact type with human feedback round-trip confirmed; Gate 4 PASS

rows.title: O2/KR1 TAD Upgrade Lifecycle — Migration Manifest Schema + tad.sh Stale-File Cleanup
rows.summary: IDEA-20260609-upgrade-lifecycle-system documents a structural gap: tad.sh copies new files but never deletes stale ones (renamed/removed files persist on remote users' installs indefinitely). No migration manifest format exists. This is absent from the backlog. The principles.md 'Never Hand-Write What an Existing Tool Already Does' and deny-list principles both point to this as an infrastructure gap that compounds with every release.
rows.cost_rationale: 2-4 files modified (tad.sh + schema template + first manifest); medium complexity because tad.sh is a critical path file requiring careful shell portability.
rows.value_rationale: O2/KR1: addresses the 'durable checkpointing / lifecycle management' upgrade direction from KR2/KR3 prioritization matrix; remote upgrade correctness compounds across all 14 registered projects and any future GitHub distribution, making this a high-leverage infrastructure investment.
rows.deliverable: .tad/templates/migration-manifest-schema.yaml (declaring delete/rename/merge keys with examples); .tad/migrations/v2.28.0-to-v2.29.1.yaml (first real manifest covering files removed in recent versions from git log analysis); tad.sh apply_migration() function that reads a manifest and deletes/renames declared files before copying new ones; verify_install_complete extended to assert declared delete targets are absent post-upgrade.

rows.title: Surplus Burn Mode Phase 2: Budget-Loop Auto-Execution
rows.summary: Phase 1 done (53-candidate plan + JSON sidecar). Phase 2: budget-loop auto-execution + safety routing + dogfood. JSON sidecar contract ready. Also fix 2026-06-13 filename bug where date/output_path args didn't propagate.
rows.cost_rationale: New budget-loop workflow + safety routing + dogfood; substantial new code and validation
rows.value_rationale: Closes the surplus burn loop from planning to execution; high strategic value for consuming usage productively
rows.deliverable: surplus-burn-mode Phase 2 implementation: budget-loop executor consuming JSON sidecar, safety routing (safe vs needs-human), dogfood run on ≥3 candidates; SURPLUS-BURN-LOG artifact; Gate 4 PASS

rows.title: detect_state Glob-Arm Hazard Fix in tad.sh
rows.summary: tad.sh 2.1*/2.2* arms will misclassify 3-part version strings once TARGET_VERSION moves past 2.19.1. Must be addressed before next version bump.
rows.cost_rationale: Single file targeted fix in tad.sh version detection arms
rows.value_rationale: Time-sensitive: will cause misclassification at next release bump, potentially blocking upgrade detection
rows.deliverable: Updated tad.sh detect_state arms (~lines 305-313) with non-ambiguous version matching; passing tad.sh self-check; Gate 3 PASS

rows.title: O1/KR1 Framework Comparative Analysis — Consolidate Rounds 1-3 into Formal Report
rows.summary: Three rounds of deep-ask findings (2026-05-05, 2026-05-14, tad-evolution-refresh) documented TAD vs 8+ frameworks but the evidence sits in scattered per-session files. KR1 requires '≥8 frameworks analyzed' in a form that is citable and decision-grade — no single document currently satisfies that. This task synthesizes all three rounds into one structured comparative report and marks KR1 ✅.
rows.cost_rationale: Pure synthesis from existing .tad/evidence/research files — no web search, no code changes, single output document.
rows.value_rationale: O1/KR1: the only remaining gap is the absence of a consolidated, citable artifact; all raw evidence already exists across three research rounds — closing KR1 ✅ is one synthesis pass away.
rows.deliverable: .tad/evidence/research/o1-kr1-framework-comparison.md containing: (1) comparison table (≥8 frameworks × 5 dimensions: role separation, quality assurance, HITL mechanisms, memory model, evaluation approach), (2) evidence citations to existing findings files (no new web searches required), (3) TAD position row in the same table, (4) OBJECTIVES.md KR1 status updated from 🔄 to ✅.

rows.title: Triple-Question KA — Knowledge + Skill + Workflow Capture at Every Gate
rows.summary: Extend Blake's Gate 3 Knowledge Assessment and Alex's *accept (Gate 4) to ask three questions: (1) new knowledge?, (2) reusable skill candidate (Skillify)?, (3) reusable workflow candidate? Add the same trigger to *discuss exits and Workflow tool completion. Add a 5th Skillify gate: judgment-type vs orchestration-type.
rows.cost_rationale: Touches two SAFETY-adjacent SKILL.md files plus one new template — M scope with SAFETY review required.
rows.value_rationale: Directly closes the missing bottom-up accumulation loop — user explicitly flagged as high priority, and the mechanism already partially works via KA.
rows.deliverable: blake/SKILL.md knowledge_assessment section and alex/SKILL.md acceptance_protocol.step7 both updated with triple-question structure + .tad/templates/workflow-candidate-template.md (WCAND format) created

rows.title: O3/KR3 Round 5 Deep Ask — Cost Optimization + Agent Evaluation Benchmarks Beyond SWE-bench
rows.summary: OBJECTIVES.md targets ≥5 deep-ask rounds for KR3 (currently at 3; Round 4 is on the backlog). Round 5 is needed to hit ✅ and covers two topics OBJECTIVES.md explicitly names as still-needed: 'cost optimization' (prompt caching, framework overhead, token budget management) and 'agent evaluation benchmarks beyond SWE-bench' (CLEAR framework, trajectory metrics, LLM-as-Judge calibration). These topics have partial signal in Round 2 findings but were not the focus of a dedicated deep-ask session.
rows.cost_rationale: Single output findings document produced via a NotebookLM deep-ask session; no code changes, no new source curation required.
rows.value_rationale: O3/KR3: closing the 5-round target directly marks this KR ✅ and feeds O2/KR1 with two research-grounded upgrade candidates (cost-optimization and eval-benchmarks are both Tier 1 impact directions per KR2/KR3 findings that have partial but not deep coverage).
rows.deliverable: .tad/evidence/research/2026-06-{DD}-o3-kr3-round5-deep-ask-findings.md containing: ≥3 structured finding blocks (cost-optimization, eval-benchmarks, one emergent topic from notebook), each with TAD implication + cited source. OBJECTIVES.md comment updated to note 5 rounds complete, Round 5 file path recorded, KR3 status updated from 🔄 to ✅.

rows.title: Re-auth Codex + Run regen-codex-editions.sh Live Happy Path
rows.summary: Codex-Edition Parity residual: regen-codex-editions.sh live happy-path unproven due to token_revoked (401). Re-auth codex → run bash .tad/codex/regen-codex-editions.sh → review diff.
rows.cost_rationale: Single script run after auth fix; bounded verification
rows.value_rationale: Unblocks Codex edition parity guarantee that was already designed and wired; eliminates known environmental residual
rows.deliverable: Successful regen-codex-editions.sh run output; git diff reviewed and committed; codex editions at current parity; parity-check.sh exit 0

rows.title: dream-scanner Pass C Dedup + Scope Hardening
rows.summary: Three bundled Pass C issues: (a) no dedup against existing project-knowledge before emit; (b) file=null causes override candidates to misclassify as project; (c) line 183 fromjson error on malformed context leaks junk candidate.
rows.cost_rationale: Three targeted fixes in dream-scanner shell script with fixture additions
rows.value_rationale: Improves signal quality of *evolve by removing duplicate and misclassified candidates
rows.deliverable: Updated dream-scanner Pass C with dedup logic, null-file classify_scope fix, and fromjson error guard; fixture suite proving all 3 bugs fixed; Gate 3 PASS

rows.title: Structured Feedback Collector — AI-Generated HTML Evaluation Interfaces
rows.summary: TAD methodology extension: when delivering any non-code artifact (audio, video, brand, page), AI generates both the artifact AND a matching HTML feedback interface (structured options + free input + JSON export). Deliver a reusable generator pattern and one worked example based on Colin voice project prototypes.
rows.cost_rationale: New capability pack with research-backed references — Epic-phase scope given cross-domain applicability.
rows.value_rationale: Closes the human-AI creative feedback loop across all non-code domains — research validated (66 sources, 6 rounds), multiple working prototypes exist, industry window is months not years.
rows.deliverable: .tad/capability-packs/feedback-collector/ CAPABILITY.md with step-by-step generation protocol + references/html-interface-patterns.md + one end-to-end example (input: artifact type → output: HTML + JSON schema)

rows.title: Dual-Platform Orchestration Adapter — Claude Code + Codex Workflow API
rows.summary: Build detect-platform.sh (already partly done in Dynamic Workflow Epic) and a thin adapter layer that selects Claude Code Workflow JS vs Codex TOML agents + --team at session start, enabling the same TAD workflow invocations to run on both platforms.
rows.cost_rationale: New adapter workflow + platform detection + cross-platform test — Epic-phase scope.
rows.value_rationale: Directly enables TAD to run on Codex with equivalent quality — high strategic value given Codex first-class status in v2.26+.
rows.deliverable: .tad/hooks/lib/detect-platform.sh (tier detection) + codex adapter for at least 1 existing .workflow.js + passing run on both Claude Code (tier 1a) and Codex (tier 1b)

rows.title: TAD Repositioning — Judgment-Surface Reflex as Core Methodology Entry
rows.summary: Codify the 'judgment surface' reflex (when no auto-evaluator exists → generate HTML evaluation interface) as a core protocol step in Alex SKILL.md's design phase. Extract the pattern from Colin voice project (ab_votes.json, evaluate_v3.html) as a concrete project-knowledge entry. README rewrite deferred pending third-domain evidence.
rows.cost_rationale: Primarily a project-knowledge write + possible minor Alex SKILL.md update — M scope touching SAFETY-adjacent SKILL.
rows.value_rationale: Converts a repeatedly proven real-world pattern into a first-class reusable reflex, directly addressing the 'no auto-evaluator' class of domains TAD targets.
rows.deliverable: .tad/project-knowledge/ux.md updated with judgment-surface reflex entry (trigger: task domain has no auto-evaluator → generate HTML interface → export JSON) + one reference example path from Colin project

rows.title: TAD Universal Gate Non-Dev Dogfood (Colin Voice Project)
rows.summary: TAD Universal Gate (AC-driven verification) has no non-dev dogfood. First real non-dev Handoff in Colin project should validate Gate new behavior end-to-end.
rows.cost_rationale: Requires setting up a real non-dev handoff scenario in Colin project and running full Gate cycle
rows.value_rationale: Validates the universal gate works outside synthetic fixtures; prevents silent regression in non-dev track
rows.deliverable: Gate 3+4 run on a real non-dev handoff in Colin project with gate4-acceptance-report.md confirming AC-driven verification worked correctly; trace event emitted

rows.title: IDEA: Capability Pack Codex YAML Adapter (6-line openai.yaml)
rows.summary: IDEA-20260527: Add 6-line openai.yaml adapter to each capability pack so Codex can natively consume them without SKILL synthesis.
rows.cost_rationale: Template design + application to multiple packs + installer update; moderate scope
rows.value_rationale: Improves Codex platform parity for capability packs; leverages completed pack-system-unification work
rows.deliverable: openai.yaml adapter template + applied to ≥3 capability packs; Codex load test showing pack loads without SKILL synthesis; pack installer updated to write .agents/openai.yaml; Gate 3 PASS

rows.title: IDEA: Automated TAD Behavior Validation Agent
rows.summary: IDEA-20260401: tad-self-test-agent — automated agent that validates TAD behavior (gates fire, agents follow protocols, skip detection).
rows.cost_rationale: New workflow + test scenarios + validation framework; significant design work
rows.value_rationale: High strategic value as TAD grows; auto-validation prevents regression in protocol adherence but confidence is low since scope is underspecified
rows.deliverable: tad-self-test-agent.workflow.js or equivalent; test suite of ≥5 behavioral scenarios (gate fires, protocol skip detected, Layer 2 triggered); run report showing ≥3 passing; Gate 4 PASS

rows.title: Add Bash Command Deny Patterns to PreToolUse Hook
rows.summary: Add destructive command patterns (rm -rf, DROP TABLE, etc.) to the PreToolUse hook for safety filtering.
rows.cost_rationale: Single hook file update with a fixture; straightforward pattern matching
rows.value_rationale: Safety improvement; but principles.md notes single-user CLI prefers soft reminders over mechanical enforcement
rows.deliverable: Updated PreToolUse hook with deny pattern list for destructive commands; fixture proving rm -rf and DROP TABLE are blocked with clear error message; Gate 3 PASS

rows.title: EPIC: Goal-Driven Research Director — Phase 3 (Research-Decision Loop)
rows.summary: Phase 3 of 4: Research-Decision Loop with decision traceability and --caller flag. Phases 1-2 done, Phase 4 already complete (P4+5+6A shipped).
rows.cost_rationale: Single Epic phase; moderate scope touching research SKILL and trace schema
rows.value_rationale: Closes the gap between research findings and actionable decisions; completes an Epic with 3/4 phases done
rows.deliverable: Research-Decision Loop protocol in research-notebook SKILL with --caller flag; decision traceability linking research findings to specific handoff decisions; Gate 3+4 PASS; Epic archived

rows.title: Surplus Burn Mode Phase 1.1: Fix 2026-06-13 filename + AC1 verifier defect
rows.summary: Carry-forward P2 fixes from Phase 1 acceptance: SKILL owns the dated output filename, and the node --check AC verifier is replaced with a wrapped-body check.
rows.cost_rationale: Two isolated bug fixes in a single skill file and one workflow file — no new abstraction needed.
rows.value_rationale: Prevents every *surplus --plan run from overwriting a single 2026-06-13 file, making the tool reliably usable before Phase 2 lands.
rows.deliverable: surplus SKILL owns dated output path (no more SURPLUS-PLAN-2026-06-13.md); `node --check` replaced with wrapped-body check in surplus-scan workflow AC; epic-audit.workflow.js:80 top-level-array schema bug fixed

rows.title: SessionStart Full Component Health Check
rows.summary: Extend the SessionStart hook to verify framework component integrity at startup: hook scripts exist, config version is consistent, template files are intact. Currently only docs (handoff/NEXT.md) are checked.
rows.cost_rationale: Extends an existing hook script and adds a check script — 2-3 files, moderate scope.
rows.value_rationale: Catches broken installs immediately rather than mid-session, directly supporting the post-sync verification discipline.
rows.deliverable: .tad/hooks/session-start-health.sh extended with component checks (hook script existence, config version, template integrity) + passing test run output

rows.title: TAD Unified Auth Layer — tad auth status Command
rows.summary: Build a tad auth status command that queries all 4 TAD-dependent tools (gh, codex, gemini, notebooklm) using normalized checks and outputs a unified table. Integrate into Alex/Blake startup to surface stale auth before mid-session failures.
rows.cost_rationale: New script + hook integration, 2-3 files — M scope.
rows.value_rationale: Directly addresses repeated login failures blocking research workflows; solves the Aborted! misreport and grep-fails-on-new-format class of bugs.
rows.deliverable: .tad/hooks/lib/auth-status.sh that outputs a normalized 4-row table (tool / mechanism / status / last-checked) + integration into session-start-health.sh so it runs at Alex/Blake boot

rows.title: Run *evolve Cross-Project (5 projects, 50+ trace files)
rows.summary: Pending: run *evolve across 5 projects that have traces (50+ total files) to surface cross-project learning.
rows.cost_rationale: Cross-project run involves reading 50+ trace files; moderate token cost
rows.value_rationale: First real cross-project *evolve run with V2 traces; validates the full trace→dream→evolve pipeline on real data
rows.deliverable: *evolve cross-project report with ≥2 candidates surfaced for promotion to principles.md or patterns/; at least 1 pattern added with ≥2-project evidence

rows.title: EPIC: Agent Capability Packs — Remaining 3/9 Phases
rows.summary: Phase 2 (real project validation in menu-snap), Phase 3 (cross-agent validation on Codex), Phase 4 (template extraction with CONSUMES/PRODUCES standard) remain from the 9-phase Epic.
rows.cost_rationale: Multi-phase Epic with cross-project and cross-platform validation; significant scope
rows.value_rationale: Foundational pack validation work; but partially superseded by pack-system-unification changes and newer pack builds
rows.deliverable: Phase 2: quality delta report from menu-snap with pack usage measured. Phase 3: same packs validated on Codex. Phase 4: CONSUMES/PRODUCES template extracted and applied to all 8 packs. Epic archived.

rows.title: classify_scope Word-Boundary Slug Matching Fix
rows.summary: Unbounded substring globs *hook*/*trace*/*registry* false-classify project slugs as framework (e.g., webhook-handler→framework). Fix = bracket-class word-boundary per architecture.md pattern.
rows.cost_rationale: Small targeted fix in one shell function with a fixture
rows.value_rationale: Low risk of framework candidates incorrectly fanning out cross-project in *evolve; bounded fix
rows.deliverable: Updated classify_scope function using word-boundary patterns; fixture proving webhook-handler classified as project not framework; Gate 3 PASS

rows.title: Agent-Adjacent Pack Citation Pointer Audit
rows.summary: 8 agent-adjacent packs: findings.md source-lists and report-body reference lists have DIFFERENT [N] numbering; pack Source: [N] tags may point to wrong URLs. Claims correct but pointers ambiguous — audit + pin URLs.
rows.cost_rationale: Audit across 8 pack files; moderate cross-referencing work
rows.value_rationale: Research auditability improvement; aligns with principles.md YOLO audit findings on research evidence lacking auditability
rows.deliverable: All 8 agent-adjacent pack findings.md files with unified [N] numbering and verified Source: [N] URL pointers; diff showing corrections; Gate 3 PASS

rows.title: Capability Pack Codex YAML Adapter — Auto-Generate openai.yaml per Pack
rows.summary: Add --agent=codex flag to each capability pack's install.sh that auto-generates a 6-line agents/openai.yaml from the pack's SKILL.md frontmatter, giving TAD packs zero-cost Codex compatibility without maintaining a separate AGENTS.md per pack.
rows.cost_rationale: Touches install.sh template and up to 25 pack install scripts — 3-4 files for template, M scope.
rows.value_rationale: Closes the install-layer platform gap identified in pack-system-unification; Codex adapter validation confirmed these adapters work.
rows.deliverable: install.sh template updated with --agent=codex flag + openai.yaml generator function + one pack fully migrated with grep-verifiable .agents/skills/{pack}/agents/openai.yaml output

rows.title: Cross-Project Skill Harvest — *harvest Command for Promoting Local Skills
rows.summary: New *harvest command in Alex that scans all registered projects' local skill directories (via sync-registry.yaml), generates a cross-project index table, and presents a review flow (promote / copy to project / keep local / deprecate). Integrates with the T1/T2/T3 destination taxonomy from the self-evolution-pruning Epic.
rows.cost_rationale: Requires new protocol in Alex SKILL.md plus scan script — new workflow, likely >100k tokens total including SAFETY review.
rows.value_rationale: Closes the missing reflux channel in the skillify pipeline — high alignment with the already-accepted self-evolution-pruning Epic direction.
rows.deliverable: *harvest command in .claude/skills/alex/SKILL.md with a scan step that reads sync-registry.yaml registered projects and produces a harvest-index.md listing slug/project/date/status for each candidate

rows.title: Multi-table §11 Decision Parser Re-bind
rows.summary: emit_decision_points locks havehdr on FIRST Decision Summary table and never re-binds; 2nd+ decision tables in one §11 are silently dropped. Fix = re-bind havehdr when fresh row cells match decision+chosen.
rows.cost_rationale: Single hook lib function fix with one fixture; pre-existing low-frequency bug
rows.value_rationale: Low priority per NEXT.md (multi-table §11 rare), but clean data quality fix with bounded scope
rows.deliverable: Fixed emit_decision_points in the trace hook lib with test fixture proving multi-table §11 emits all decision rows; Gate 3 PASS

rows.title: Local Skill Capture — *save-skill Command and Directory Isolation
rows.summary: Add *save-skill command (Alex) that extracts the current session's solution pattern into a local project skill (.claude/skills/local/*). Requires spike to verify Claude Code loads skills from subdirectories, plus *sync protection to never overwrite local skills.
rows.cost_rationale: Touches Alex SKILL.md protocol and sync verification — 3-4 files, moderate scope.
rows.value_rationale: Enables bottom-up capability accumulation, complementing the research-driven top-down pack building.
rows.deliverable: *save-skill command in .claude/skills/alex/SKILL.md + one verified test: a local skill created via *save-skill is loaded in a subsequent session and *sync does not overwrite it

rows.title: Thin Protocol + Thick Tools Audit — SKILL.md Judgment vs Orchestration Separation
rows.summary: Audit alex/SKILL.md and blake/SKILL.md to identify which protocol steps are orchestration logic (can move to .workflow.js) vs judgment rules (must stay in SKILL body). Produce a classification table and a Phase 1 migration of 1-2 identified orchestration sections to workflow scripts.
rows.cost_rationale: Read-heavy audit plus targeted SKILL.md edits — SAFETY-adjacent (touches alex/blake SKILL), M-L boundary.
rows.value_rationale: Reduces SKILL.md context bloat and moves TAD toward the platform-native workflow direction validated by the dynamic-workflow Epic.
rows.deliverable: .tad/evidence/designs/thin-protocol-audit.md with per-section classification (judgment vs orchestration) for alex/SKILL.md + at least 1 section migrated to a .workflow.js with Gate 3 PASS

rows.title: Express Tier Durable Frontmatter Marker
rows.summary: Slug-naming convention for express handoffs still false-WARNs when express is forgotten in slug. Durable fix = express: true frontmatter consumed by layer2-audit instead of slug-as-proxy.
rows.cost_rationale: Requires changes to layer2-audit script plus handoff template frontmatter schema
rows.value_rationale: Reduces false positive noise in layer2-audit, improving signal quality for express workflow
rows.deliverable: layer2-audit updated to read express: true frontmatter from handoff YAML; test fixture proving no false-WARN on express handoff without express-in-slug; Gate 3 PASS

rows.title: IDEA: Unified Agent Detection + Invocation Protocol
rows.summary: IDEA-20260527: Unified agent detection + invocation protocol as TAD cross-agent running infrastructure.
rows.cost_rationale: New protocol design + implementation + dual-platform testing; significant scope
rows.value_rationale: Infrastructure enabling TAD to run consistently across platforms but scope is underspecified in the idea
rows.deliverable: agent-adapter.md protocol spec + detect_agent_platform() shell function; integration in tad.sh; smoke test on Claude Code + Codex showing correct platform detection and routing; Gate 3 PASS

rows.title: TAD Self-Test Agent — Automated Agent Behavior Verification
rows.summary: Create a sub-agent that automatically verifies TAD functionality after upgrades or syncs: reads config files, invokes /alex//blake via Skill tool, checks hook triggers and Domain Pack loading. Deliverable: .tad/tests/self-test.sh or a sub-agent task definition that produces a PASS/FAIL report against a fixed checklist.
rows.cost_rationale: Requires spike on sub-agent Skill-tool nesting limits plus test harness authoring — likely >100k tokens.
rows.value_rationale: Reduces manual regression burden after every upgrade/sync, but nested Skill-tool invocation is technically uncertain.
rows.deliverable: .tad/tests/self-test-agent.md task definition + sample PASS/FAIL run output against ≥5 checklist items

rows.title: Unified Agent Adapter Pattern — TAD Cross-Platform Runtime Detection
rows.summary: Build a detect-agent.sh script that auto-detects available coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI) via PATH scanning, announces capability tier at session start, and selects the appropriate orchestration adapter. Modeled on html-anything's AgentDef + detect.ts pattern.
rows.cost_rationale: New workflow / adapter library touching hooks and potentially SKILL.md — likely >100k tokens.
rows.value_rationale: Supports the TAD universal method direction and cross-platform portability, but multi-turn protocol portability is much harder than single-turn html-anything.
rows.deliverable: .tad/hooks/lib/detect-agent.sh that outputs tier (1a/1b/2/3) + agent name at session start, with passing detection test on Claude Code and Codex

rows.title: Run *optimize on menu-snap (14 trace files)
rows.summary: Pending: run *optimize analysis on menu-snap project which has 14 trace files to analyze execution patterns.
rows.cost_rationale: Single command run with analysis; bounded to reading existing trace files
rows.value_rationale: Validates *optimize works on a real multi-trace project; may surface cross-project patterns
rows.deliverable: *optimize output report for menu-snap with execution pattern analysis and at least 1 actionable finding added to project-knowledge or principles.md

rows.title: Semantic Dedup for Dream-Scanner Candidates
rows.summary: grep-on-.decision/.chosen dedup is inert (0/31 real values match). Needs title/discovery match or embedding-based semantic dedup to catch near-duplicate candidates.
rows.cost_rationale: Requires designing and implementing a dedup algorithm; embedding approach would need external dependency
rows.value_rationale: Low confidence since the dedup approach needs design; current false-positive rate from missing dedup is low-frequency
rows.deliverable: Dream-scanner with title/discovery string-similarity dedup (e.g., normalized Levenshtein or token overlap); fixture proving near-duplicate candidates are merged; Gate 3 PASS

rows.title: IDEA: Dream Scanner Auto-Scope via Git Remote Hash
rows.summary: IDEA-20260527: Dream scanner auto-scope using git remote hash + 2-project promotion threshold to avoid manual scope specification.
rows.cost_rationale: Moderate shell scripting changes to dream-scanner plus git integration
rows.value_rationale: Reduces manual overhead in *evolve but confidence is low since the mechanism design is underspecified
rows.deliverable: Updated dream-scanner with git-remote-hash-based scope detection; 2-project promotion logic; fixture proving project vs framework scope is auto-detected correctly; Gate 3 PASS

rows.title: Hook Timeout Configuration
rows.summary: Add per-hook-type timeout_seconds field to TAD's Claude Code settings.json hook definitions to prevent infinite waits if a hook script hangs. Deliverable: updated .claude/settings.json with timeout fields + verification that Claude Code honors them.
rows.cost_rationale: Single-file settings change plus a short spike to verify native behavior — under 30k tokens.
rows.value_rationale: Low-risk defensive improvement, but Claude Code may already have built-in timeouts making this redundant.
rows.deliverable: .claude/settings.json hook entries updated with timeout_seconds per hook type + one-paragraph finding on whether Claude Code natively honors this field

rows.title: Test Agent Teams on Next Full or Standard TAD Task
rows.summary: Pending: Test Agent Teams feature on the next Full or Standard TAD task to validate the workflow.
rows.cost_rationale: Requires a real task to run; moderate overhead for the comparison documentation
rows.value_rationale: Low confidence since Agent Teams value is unproven; but could unlock parallel execution benefits if it works
rows.deliverable: Gate 4 acceptance report for a Full/Standard TAD task completed using Agent Teams; with comparison notes on quality vs single-agent execution

rows.title: Stale Mention Sweep from Self-Evolution Pruning
rows.summary: Carry-forward from Self-Evolution Pruning: stale mentions in intent-router-protocol.md L150/L198 + accept-command.md L251 + handoff-a-to-b.md L24; alex SKILL header references missing AR-extract fixture path.
rows.cost_rationale: Small targeted text fixes in a handful of doc files
rows.value_rationale: Cosmetic doc hygiene; prevents confusion but minimal functional impact
rows.deliverable: Updated intent-router-protocol.md, accept-command.md, handoff-a-to-b.md with correct references; alex SKILL header updated with AR-extract fixture path; verified no remaining stale mentions

rows.title: Add --force No-op to academic-research and research-methodology Installers
rows.summary: 2 pack installers (academic-research, research-methodology) reject --force. Sync script workarounds with fallback but the installers should accept --force as no-op for consistency.
rows.cost_rationale: Two small installer script edits; trivial change
rows.value_rationale: Minor consistency fix; sync script already has a workaround so functional impact is near zero
rows.deliverable: Updated install.sh for academic-research and research-methodology accepting --force flag as no-op; verified via --force invocation returning exit 0; Gate 3 PASS

rows.title: Domain Pack Monthly Refresh — Tools & Skills Periodic Update
rows.summary: A monthly health-check handoff template for Blake: scan tools-registry.yaml for stale versions, search for new MCP servers/CLI tools released in the past month, and produce an updated registry + changelog entry.
rows.cost_rationale: Single template file, no new abstraction — small token cost.
rows.value_rationale: Keeps tool recommendations current, but Domain Pack YAML is partially superseded by Capability Packs, limiting actual impact.
rows.deliverable: .tad/templates/domain-pack-refresh-handoff.md — a reusable Blake handoff template with WebSearch steps and a changelog output spec

rows.title: Add TAD Merge Marker to 3 Merge-Strategy Projects
rows.summary: 3 projects (my-openclaw-agents, toy, 内存管理) lack <!-- TAD:PROJECT-CONTENT-BELOW --> marker; sync script warned but left untouched. Add marker or switch to overwrite strategy.
rows.cost_rationale: Small edits to 3 downstream project CLAUDE.md files
rows.value_rationale: Eliminates sync warnings; ensures future TAD releases propagate correctly to these projects
rows.deliverable: TAD:PROJECT-CONTENT-BELOW marker added to CLAUDE.md in 3 downstream projects (or documented decision to switch to overwrite); sync re-run confirming no warn for those projects

rows.title: Config Environment Variable Override (4-Layer Priority)
rows.summary: Add env-var override layer to TAD config loading (default → file → env → CLI), enabling CI/CD or container deployments to override settings without touching YAML. Deliverable: updated config loader script + naming convention doc (TAD_MODEL, TAD_CONFIG_PATH).
rows.cost_rationale: Touches config loading logic and documentation — 2-3 files, moderate scope.
rows.value_rationale: Useful mainly for CI/CD scenarios, but TAD is primarily single-user CLI with little CI/CD demand today.
rows.deliverable: .tad/hooks/lib/config-loader.sh with _apply_env_overrides logic + naming convention entry in .tad/project-knowledge/patterns/shell-portability.md

rows.title: Dream Scanner Auto-Scope via Git Remote Hash
rows.summary: Extend dream-scanner.sh to auto-detect scope_tag (project vs framework) by hashing the git remote URL as a project ID, then auto-suggesting scope_tag: framework when the same candidate appears across ≥2 registered projects in sync-registry.yaml.
rows.cost_rationale: Modifies 1-2 existing shell scripts with moderate complexity.
rows.value_rationale: Automates a manual step but dream loop has near-zero acceptance rate per self-evolution-pruning measurement — improving the auto-scope of a low-yield pipeline has limited ROI.
rows.deliverable: dream-scanner.sh updated with git-remote-hash project ID + cross-project pattern dedup step that outputs scope_tag: framework recommendation when ≥2 project hits detected

rows.title: GitAgent Protocol (GAP) Export for TAD Cross-Platform Portability
rows.summary: Map TAD's .tad/ directory structure to GitAgent Protocol (agent.yaml, memory/, skills/, hooks/) and produce a GAP-compatible export that enables TAD skills to run on non-Claude runtimes. Note: GAP spec is still v0.1.0.
rows.cost_rationale: New abstraction layer touching multiple framework directories — Epic-phase scope.
rows.value_rationale: Supports TAD universal method direction but GAP is pre-stable (v0.1.0) and the multi-turn protocol mapping is unsolved.
rows.deliverable: .tad/gap-export/ directory with agent.yaml mapping + at least 2 TAD skills exported in GAP format + validation that GAP spec accepts the output structure

rows.title: Domain Pack Taxonomy Reorg — Vertical vs Cross-Cutting Dimensions
rows.summary: Add cross_cutting: true tags to relevant packs in keywords.yaml and a classification index in README, without renaming existing YAML files. Low-cost alternative to full taxonomy reorg.
rows.cost_rationale: Single-file or two-file change with no new abstraction.
rows.value_rationale: No confirmed user pain point; demoted from Epic explicitly because no concrete 'can't find the pack' evidence exists.
rows.deliverable: keywords.yaml updated with cross_cutting: true on ≥3 relevant packs + cross-cutting index section appended to .tad/domains/README.md
